tokens: Okay; here we go; rolling again.
Okay; we'll try getting home on the Nav system here.
Oh, look at that big fresh one in the side of the rim!
(Garbled) that stereo pan, so we're right up...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Got to make a...Don't want to go (directly) downslope too much.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Looks like it (the navigation system) takes us right back the way we came, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're going to have to go to the right to go around Elbow.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, yeah; I was suggesting that we could go to the left, but I guess we don't want to do that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't think we'd better try it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) That's a neat place down into the rille though, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See the way it comes back?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: down into the rille, Joe, (lost under Joe) would like...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...we'd rather that you don't take that option.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And if anybody ever comes back, Joe, and wants to go down into the rille, have them come talk to us, because there's a good place to do it here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; we're moving at about 5 clicks (km/hr).
And the slope, I'd guess is about 6 or 7 degrees; and we're going cross-slope.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to go down this way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we better go down to...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...a slope that's not quite as steep.
I didn't realize we'd gone up so high.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, looking back, man, we really climbed it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Come back up here (that is, angle more to the east so that they are not headed directly down the slope).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we know our tracks are to the right of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...any idea of whether you can see the LM or not?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I took a look when we were up there (at Station 2), and I couldn't see it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And if you cross over Rover tracks, Dave, we'd like a depth estimation of them, please (for use by the soil mechanics and Rover traction experts).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Responding to Jim's joke) Oh, mercy, yes (laughter).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Got to go easy downhill, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'd say so (both laugh; almost giggle).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Unaware of the reason for the laughter) It's uphill from here on in.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There's a hill we have to climb here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You just...You can't go fast downhill in this thing, because if you try and turn with the front wheels locked up like that, they dig in and the rear end breaks away, and around you go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Tell Jim it must be that powdered material on the slope there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; we're down (on to the mare).
It's fairly level now and we're going to start upslope (out of a trough at the base of the mountain); and we're just about on the south rim of Elbow.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, that's probably the first christy...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: If we get back up here...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, now we're up to 9 clicks; you have to swing to the right here, Dave, whenever you can.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We want to get up on the ridge line here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And also stay away from up-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Up-Sun isn't too bad though, you know?
There's a lot more definition than straight down-Sun.
I don't think we'll have any trouble driving up-Sun, because the craters seem to show up pretty well.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Have you noticed here on Elbow, it seems like there's a very subtle bench on the southern side?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I kind of got the idea there were several subtle benches in the downslope, particularly on the eastern wall.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We just crossed over a buried, rounded boulder.
Must've been a meter and a half across, with...Of course, it's all gray.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gets pretty rough up ahead, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, they really don't sink in very far.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'd say less than a half an inch, if that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Maybe you can find a smoother way home.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I'm going to do (means 'follow') the Nav system here, once we get squared away and get out of the hole.
(To Houston) Incidentally, Joe, I don't think we saw any indication of flows or a slide or anything coming off of Hadley Delta there.
I didn't see anything that looked like a change in granularity or any subtle scarps of any sort.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, that's exactly the picture we built from your verbal description there and that's one of the reasons we're going to omit Station 3.
We think that there probably is not at least a sharp debris flow down the side.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think that's a good decision.
I don't think we're going to find much more over there than we've already found.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, looking out to the east now, Dave, I see some very subtle ridges.
I think they're ridges rather than craters.
And it's probably...Well, it's out toward the secondary crater cluster.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, see that white albedo I kicked up over there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gosh, I sure wish we had more time to sample.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I see the...I see something reflecting over there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: See the reflection of it at 12 o'clock (that is, straight ahead)?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we're heading right straight for it on a bearing of 11 degrees (laughs) - except for the wanders through the craters.
(Pause) Boy, I'll tell you, Joe, this is a super way to travel; nice and cool; uphill without any strain.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And it's easy to drive; no problem at all.
Just have to be careful because of the locked front wheels, but other than that, very responsive.
I can put the throttle right up to the stop or at some intermediate position; and take my hand off and rest my hand.
If I want to go left or right, I just put a little pressure until I get the angle I want and then let it off and we re-center on the steering.
It's really neat, even with the locked front wheels.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we're still working on your front wheel problem.
We may have them back before you know it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; I'd like to try it that way, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looking over to the east, Dave, I see a very large crater, and it could very well be...Could it be Dune?
No, it's probably too close to be Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, look at this rock right on the sur(face)...Hey, you know...See that one on the surface there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll bet you...I wouldn't be surprised if it didn't come from that crater.

(To Houston) There's a rock that was sort of rounded, but had a rough surface texture to it, about a half a meter in size; and it was about 10 meters downstream from a nice fresh crater that had a lot of angular debris in the bottom and the walls.

(Pause) There are a lot of little craters around here - 'little' being less than a meter - which are very rough; have a lot of debris right up to the rim and over the top side of the rim; and no ejecta blanket to speak of, but the whole inside of the wall...Take a half a meter crater and it's filled with angular, gray, fragmental debris on the order of inch size or less; very uniformly distributed and fairly well sorted.
Like, maybe, the debris is from one of our Aristillus or Autolycus friends.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And there's a lot of it, so I think we'll have a chance to get it later on.
Let's say that they're only about, oh, 1 to 6 (that is, the depth is only one-sixth the diameter, suggestive of a low-velocity impact).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, look; there's a large flat rock over at 1 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Concentration in this one area; and then there's a large one down in the pit of that subdued crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How we doing on time there, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It had...It almost looked like pahoehoe, didn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) How we doing on time, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, you'll be about 20 minutes down from the original plan when you get back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're not in bad shape at all.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And we may be in better shape when you arrive there.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; we'll see how we do.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Every once in a while, when one of the steering (that is, rear) wheels comes off the ground due to bumping in a crater, and we get a side force, why, the rear end will break out, because we've lost our directional control.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, did you comment on the
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There are two or three of them right at the base.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It was all in the shadow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, there's definitely a horizontal pattern in the spur of Hadley.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh shoot, it really gets bumpy.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And then as you go up above that - and, again, that's maybe only 10, 15 percent of that particular exposure of the spur - then there's a definitely linear pattern that looks like it dips 30 degrees to the west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I got to put my seatbelt on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, stand(ing) by for mark when you start.
Help us on our speed calculations.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yes, I'm sorry about that, Joe, but I'm pretty unstable without that seatbelt and there's a lot of feedback into the controller.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; Mark (as they start rolling again).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Anytime we stop, Joe, I'll let you know (Laughs).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) I was going to tell you, but your descriptions were so neat.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, it's nice to be able to see the LM.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, and keep talking; the description's beautiful.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We can see several craters on Hadley.
Hard to estimate what the size of them is, but the ones that I can resolve seem to be a fairly uniform size, that I can resolve from this distance.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, there's a rough area ahead.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'd like to see Rhysling on the way in.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We gave you a mark when we started, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now this large one ahead could be...No, we're not close enough yet to be Rhysling.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Look at this boulder here, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; we're coming out across either an elongate crater or two that are kind of joined up - running east-west.
Kind of a doublet, and we're going across the bridge between them.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And it must be, maybe 30 meters across on each one of them with no debris and they're smooth on the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we've got them on the map.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, there's some vesicular basalt right there, boy.
Let's just hold on one second, we've got to have...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you hand me your seatbelt?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) If you'll hang on to it here for a second.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll let you know when we move.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, Joe, these small fresh craters that we've commented on, whatever caused them must create...
(being more precise) or indurate the soil into the rocks - (that is) creates its own rocks (regolith breccia), because there's just a concentration of rocks around the very fresh ones (craters).
And by 'small' I'm talking about may be a foot to 3 feet diameter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, ready to hand me my (seatbelt)...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Get it (probably the piece of basalt)?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think I dropped the map.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Long Pause; sound of Dave's struggle to get the map)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, the map's so dirty you can hardly see it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Better tighten this thing up a little bit better.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's a pretty fresh one right up ahead, Jim.
Looks like about 10 meters across, and it's got up to 6-inch frags around the rim.
Maybe 15, 20 percent of the rim has frags in it but no significant ejecta blanket - which I think is typical of all these around here.
That one looks like it's maybe a meter and a half deep.
Nah, we can't get in it, and I bet it has glass in it, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, you can almost tell the ones that are going to have glass...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...by looking at them before you get there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, we were up to about 11 or 12 clicks on that last burst.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Roger, Jim; I'm standing by for amps readings.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, right now we're going at 10 clicks, and I'm reading about 10 amps.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Still no reading on the motor temps.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're 1.7 (from the LM) so Rhysling is...We should be near Rhysling.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: In fact, we ought to be...Rhysling ought to be off to our right.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, there's a pretty sharp one right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're reading 013 to the LM, and we're at 1.6.
We ought to be able to see Rhysling.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) It should be about a 125-meter crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Gee, I don't see it, do you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, there's one over here at 2 o'clock that's fairly deep and might...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's deep, but it's not near that big.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Haven't really seen any large enough that we'd call Rhysling, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, Jim, it may just be...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Occasionally as we drop down in these...You know, I kind of get the impression, Dave, it's almost like...Well, there's depressions and then there's the rises, and they're generally perpendicular to our direction of travel.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now that you mention it, you're right.
Sure does seem that way, doesn't it?
We're just going up and down the...(Chuckles) Now what's the frequency (that is, how often do they encounter depressions and rises)?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Like just small valleys that are trending upslope, Joe.
And we go down low enough so that we can't see the LM anymore.
Won't see it until we get on top of the next rise.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And they're about - would you say - 60, 70 meters across?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, you know, the terrain, looking from the east (means 'to the east'), is just a general rise to the east.
It looks like, oh, 2 or 3 percent.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Right to the base of the Apennines.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Now when we go out on EVA-2, why, it'll be uphill going out, and probably downhill all the way back.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, what a great suspension system this thing has.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It'll be a tremendous view back to the LM when we get out to the last station on EVA-2 (planned for the west rim of Front Crater at AU.7/87.0).
(Long Pause) And rather than this being the 'plains', as such, I get the idea it's a base of a very gentle talus slope.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're not on a flat plain; it looks like it slopes down from the
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, there might be a rise, you know, there at the rille.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But you're definitely right, we're traveling on a slope (which is down) to the left right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, boy, you really get that impression if you, you know, look east, look up-Sun.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) I can't believe we came over those mountains.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's just a beautiful little valley.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) Yeah, those are pretty big mountains to fly over, aren't they?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here's a nice, subtle crater about 70 meters across, with a sharp, 15-meter one on the rim which scattered debris out.
But no big ejecta blanket, no rays.
(Pause) We can't see the LM now.
(Pause) And we're traveling at about 12 clicks.
(Long Pause) Let's see, 018 degrees (bearing to the LM) for 0.7 (km range), so right over the next rise, we should see home plate.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I think I see, on the surface here, lineaments that are trending about northwest/southeast, Jim.
(Pause) Look as we go across here and if you think about them, if you look down there...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look right ahead of us here where we're driving, I see lineaments that are parallel with our direction of...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Houston) Came up over the rise, Joe, and right when we got over the rise, there was a (laughing) great big crater in our path.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (You) got some bad news and some good news.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) But it's the only game in town!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, Joe, I wish we could stop and pick up hundreds of rocks; there's so many.
There's a little one sitting on the rim of a crater that's on a pedestal.
It looks like a smooth, gray rock, subangular, and it was sitting up on a pedestal it looked like.
Right on the very rim of the crater, and it was the only frag near the crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Sitting down there in that hole.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughs) Hey, look at the...Would you think that the albedo (percentage reflectivity)'s - oop!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Is this probably Index over here to the right, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: [If] that's Index, our position is...I guess we're just a little east then...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...I think that the position they have picked out is pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's close, but it looks like it might be a little south.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, Joe, our Nav system's starting to wander now (that is, the bearing is beginning to change as they get very close to the LM and the relative errors become large).
We got a range of 0.2 (km) and a bearing of 34 and we're heading about 015 into the LM, and it's just almost in front of us.
I think it (the Nav system)'s done very well though.
(Pause) Hey, do you know I think I almost landed in a crater, Jim.
Look at the one on the right (east) there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If I'd have just gone down another...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I have that on the map.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like it's just west, northwest of Index.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay; come back here, and we want to park it (perhaps looking at CDR-15) cross-Sun, heading towards the ALSEP side (of the LM).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I landed right in a little bench there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just on the northwest rim of that crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I was hoping...I think I saw that big crater; I was hoping by keeping like a foot per second forward all the way in, it'd keep us out of something like that.
(Pause) Joe, we're back at the LM, by the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll park out here a little ways, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's a lot of glass fragments around here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: More than I've seen any other...Yeah.
If that's Index over there and this is the one that's northwest of Index, gee, that puts us at...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That puts us really at position D.
Let's see that'd be 75.5 and Baker Queen.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, and, you know, I can see why, now.
Because Index is so subtle, and there's another one that is just to the north of Salyut, which I was going to call the landing site.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're certainly very close there, and we can sort out the details of that later.
Dave, when you turn on the TV, could you police the TV cables for us again?
We think that we may be hung up again.
And we need a status check from both of your EMUs, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay; here are the Rover readings: 315 (heading), 059 (bearing to the LM), 10.3 (distance traveled, in kilometers), 00.1 (range from the LM), 100, 107 (amp hours remaining in the batteries), 95, and 95 (battery temperatures), and motor temps are both lower limit.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Joe, my EMU flag...(With a slight scolding tone in his voice) Careful, Jim, come on.
(Long Pause) (Concerned) Okay, everything nice and easy now, huh?
Let me look at your camera.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and while you're brushing it, Dave, we need a PLSS check.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Turn around here, Jim, let me get your...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm...I'm 3.85 on pressure; flags are clear, and I'm reading 45 percent.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's see your camera so when you take those pictures it's not...(Brief Pause) It's alright.
transcript.speaker: Scott
